title: Centaurium divaricatum
taxonomy: Centaurium divaricatum belongs to the family Gentianaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the gentian family. This plant is a species of gentian, specifically within the genus Centaurium. The genus Centaurium is further classified under the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The order Gentianales is itself classified under the subclass Asteridae, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Campanulaceae and Lamiaceae. The subclass Asteridae is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants that are also known as dicotyledons.

intro: Centaurium divaricatum, also known as knotted centaury, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium divaricatum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centaurium divaricatum is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried herb extract, and is commonly used in the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
